content.problemStatement:
You have NNN sticks of length 1,2,...,N1, 2, ..., N1,2,...,N respectively.

Can you make a non-degenerate triangle†^{\dagger}† with some 333 sticks out of these NNN sticks? Find the maximum possible perimeter‡^{\ddagger}‡ of a triangle you can make, or print −1-1−1 if not possible.

†^{\dagger}† You can make a non-degenerate triangle with sticks of sizes AAA, BBB and CCC if and only if 2⋅max⁡(A,B,C)<A+B+C2 \cdot \max(A, B, C) < A + B + C2⋅max(A,B,C)<A+B+C.

‡^{\ddagger}‡ The perimeter of a triangle made with sticks of lengths AAA, BBB and CCC is A+B+CA + B + CA+B+C.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of input contains NNN - the number of sticks.


Output Format
For each test case, output the maximum perimeter or −1-1−1 if not possible.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : You can make a triangle with the sticks of sies 444, 333 and 222. This has the maximum perimeter possible of 4+3+2=94 + 3 + 2 = 94+3+2=9.
Test Case 2 : No triangle is possible.
